title: T-079 — Migration/API contract: factory_specs Technical-owned version approval
prompt:
# T-079 — factory_specs Technical-owned version approval

Implement the data/API foundation for Technical-owned `factory_specs` per PRD §0 and §5.0. Create `factory_specs` with org RLS, unique `(org_id,item_id,version)`, statuses `draft|in_review|approved_for_factory|superseded|blocked`, source `technical|npd_builder|d365_import|migration`, immutable approved versions, approval metadata, effective dates, and JSON spec body. Add service/API operations: create draft, get versions, submit for review, approve for factory use, block, and edit released spec by cloning to next draft version. Active factory use requires `approved_for_factory`. RED first: tests prove approved version cannot be updated in place and edit creates version+1 draft. Do not build UI.

pipeline_inputs.details: This task establishes factory_spec as Technical-owned SSOT for production instructions. NPD Builder is allowed to call create draft with source npd_builder, but it cannot approve for factory use. Product-facing aliases may say internal_product_spec; table/API naming is factory_specs unless existing code conventions demand a thin alias. Approval must write audit_log entries. D365 imports may create draft or review records only. No application UI in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Given an approved_for_factory factory_spec exists, when update-in-place is attempted, then the operation is rejected and audit_log records the rejection.
Given an approved_for_factory version v2 exists, when editReleasedFactorySpec is called, then version v3 is created with status draft and v2 remains unchanged.
Given NPD Builder creates an initial spec, when source=npd_builder, then status is draft and approval fields are empty.
Given a factory use check runs, when no approved_for_factory spec exists for FG/intermediate, then factory use is blocked with a typed reason.
test_strategy: Add RED tests for immutable approved rows and clone-on-edit before migration/service code.
risk_red_lines: Do not merge factory_specs into items.ext_jsonb.
Do not allow NPD roles to approve factory use.
Do not make D365 authoritative for approved specs.
pipeline_inputs.description: Implement/document Migration/API contract: factory_specs Technical-owned version approval for 03-TECHNICAL final-decision workflow.
